Turner said that to him it seemed the "only country" to have a "special relationship" with the United States was "probably Israel

items.title: King Charles argues for stronger cooperation in speech to a joint meeting of Congress
items.standfirst: In an address before a joint meeting of Congress on Tuesday, King Charles III sought to highlight the importance of the transatlantic relationship between the United States and Great Britain.
items.content: In an address before a joint meeting of Congress on Tuesday, King Charles III sought to highlight the importance of the transatlantic relationship between the United States and Great Britain.

items.title: King Charles gets ovation for Congress speech warning of volatile world
items.standfirst: He tells US lawmakers his address comes the two nations' relationship is "more important" than ever "in times of great uncertainty".
items.content: He tells US lawmakers his address comes the two nations' relationship is "more important" than ever "in times of great uncertainty".

items.title: 'You'd be speaking French': King Charles pokes fun at Trump during state dinner
items.standfirst: Britain's King Charles III used his speech at a state dinner at the White House on Tuesday to poke fun at US President Donald Trump who, in January, told European leaders that without US aid in World War II they would be speaking German. "Dare I say that, if it wasn't for us, you'd be speaking French," Charles quipped.
items.content: Britain's King Charles III used his speech at a state dinner at the White House on Tuesday to poke fun at US President Donald Trump who, in January, told European leaders that without US aid in World War II they would be speaking German. "Dare I say that, if it wasn't for us, you'd be speaking French," Charles quipped.

items.title: White House hails ‘two kings’ as King Charles delivers pointed remarks
items.standfirst: King Charles III has hailed US-UK ties at a state dinner in the White House.
items.content: King Charles III has hailed US-UK ties at a state dinner in the White House.

items.title: Fashion, billionaires and jokes: Inside the White House state dinner for the King and Queen
items.standfirst: The night was filled with designer fashion, luscious bouquets, billionaire guests and joke-filled speeches.
items.content: The night was filled with designer fashion, luscious bouquets, billionaire guests and joke-filled speeches.

items.title: King Charles toasts U.S.-U.K. alliance in state dinner, after rare address to Congress
items.standfirst: King Charles attended a state dinner at the White House, after speaking about what he called the "truly unique" relationship between the U.K. and the U.S. in an address to Congress.
items.content: King Charles attended a state dinner at the White House, after speaking about what he called the "truly unique" relationship between the U.K. and the U.S. in an address to Congress.

items.title: Middle East war live: Trump says Iran has been 'militarily defeated' at state dinner
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump told Britain's King Charles III and other guests at a state dinner Tuesday that Iran has been "militarily defeated", in his first public comments on the sensitive topic during the ongoing royal visit. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.
items.content: US President Donald Trump told Britain's King Charles III and other guests at a state dinner Tuesday that Iran has been "militarily defeated", in his first public comments on the sensitive topic during the ongoing royal visit. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.

items.title: The state visit in pictures
items.standfirst: King Charles III and Queen Camilla continued their state visit to the US with a White House state dinner and a speech from the King to Congress.
items.content: King Charles III and Queen Camilla continued their state visit to the US with a White House state dinner and a speech from the King to Congress.

items.title: King Charles tells Congress that alliance is "more important today" than ever
items.standfirst: Saying he felt the "weight of history" on his shoulders, King Charles became the first British monarch in 35 years to address Congress on Tuesday.
items.content: Saying he felt the "weight of history" on his shoulders, King Charles became the first British monarch in 35 years to address Congress on Tuesday.

items.title: Only "special relationship" U.S. has is "probably Israel," U.K. ambassador said
items.standfirst: Britain's ambassador, in February remarks reported by the Financial Times, also called the lack of fallout from the Jeffrey Epstein scandal in the U.S. "extraordinary."
items.content: Britain's ambassador, in February remarks reported by the Financial Times, also called the lack of fallout from the Jeffrey Epstein scandal in the U.S. "extraordinary."

items.title: King Charles addresses Congress after British ambassador's comments about U.S. and Israel leak
items.standfirst: King Charles addressed Congress Tuesday amid tension in the so-called special relationship between the United States and the United Kingdom. Earlier in the day, private comments by the British Ambassador to the U.S., Sir Christian Turner, leaked to the Financial Times. Turner said that to him it seemed the "only country" to have a "special relationship" with the United States was "probably Israel." Ed O'Keefe has more.
items.content: King Charles addressed Congress Tuesday amid tension in the so-called special relationship between the United States and the United Kingdom. Earlier in the day, private comments by the British Ambassador to the U.S., Sir Christian Turner, leaked to the Financial Times. Turner said that to him it seemed the "only country" to have a "special relationship" with the United States was "probably Israel." Ed O'Keefe has more.

items.title: King Charles, Queen Camilla arrive at White House for state dinner
items.standfirst: President Trump hosted the U.K.'s King Charles III and Queen Camilla for a state dinner at the White House on Tuesday. White House historian Jennifer Pickens joins CBS News with more.
items.content: President Trump hosted the U.K.'s King Charles III and Queen Camilla for a state dinner at the White House on Tuesday. White House historian Jennifer Pickens joins CBS News with more.

items.title: America's special relationship 'probably Israel', says UK ambassador to US

items.content: The remarks, made in February and first reported by the Financial Times, came to light during the King's state visit.

items.title: King Charles kicks off historic trip to U.S.
items.standfirst: King Charles and Queen Camilla were given a royal welcome at the White House by President Trump. NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez reports.
items.content: King Charles and Queen Camilla were given a royal welcome at the White House by President Trump. NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez reports.

items.title: King Charles reaffirms 'special' relationship with U.S. amid tensions over Iran war
items.standfirst: King Charles III reaffirmed the United Kingdom and the United States’ long “special relationship” and common values of democracy and freedom in a rare speech to Congress on Tuesday.
items.content: King Charles III reaffirmed the United Kingdom and the United States’ long “special relationship” and common values of democracy and freedom in a rare speech to Congress on Tuesday.

items.content: New red card rule allows referees to send off players who cover their mouths or walk off the pitch in protest against a decision.

items.title: Red cards can be given to players who cover their mouths while confronting opponents
items.standfirst: The International Football Association Board approved a rule that would penalize players with a red card if they cover their mouths when confronting another player. The measure will be in place at this summer's World Cup.
items.content: The International Football Association Board approved a rule that would penalize players with a red card if they cover their mouths when confronting another player. The measure will be in place at this summer's World Cup.(Image credit: Pedro Rocha)

items.title: FIFA introduces new World Cup red-card rules to combat racism
items.standfirst: FIFA announced on Tuesday that players who cover their mouths during confrontations with opponents could be sent off at this year's World Cup under new anti-racism measures while walking off the pitch in protest at refereeing decisions may also result in red cards.
items.content: FIFA announced on Tuesday that players who cover their mouths during confrontations with opponents could be sent off at this year's World Cup under new anti-racism measures while walking off the pitch in protest at refereeing decisions may also result in red cards.

items.title: New red card offence confirmed for World Cup

bullet_summary: Reported by Al Jazeera, France24, Sky News World

items.title: Europe named ‘fastest-warming continent’ in latest climate change report
items.standfirst: From heatwaves to wildfires and shrinking ice cover, Europe is facing 'severe' impact from climate change, UN body says.
items.content: From heatwaves to wildfires and shrinking ice cover, Europe is facing 'severe' impact from climate change, UN body says.

items.title: Europe hit by record heat, glacier loss and marine extremes per climate report
items.standfirst: Europe recorded its hottest year yet in 2025, with unprecedented heatwaves stretching from the Mediterranean to the Arctic, rapid glacier melt, record sea temperatures and expanding wildfires, according to a major climate report warning that the continent is warming twice as fast as the global average.
items.content: Europe recorded its hottest year yet in 2025, with unprecedented heatwaves stretching from the Mediterranean to the Arctic, rapid glacier melt, record sea temperatures and expanding wildfires, according to a major climate report warning that the continent is warming twice as fast as the global average.

items.title: 'Dangerously high' temperatures as Europe named world's fastest warming continent
items.standfirst: Almost the entirety of Europe saw above-average temperatures last year, while wildfires burnt a record area and heatwaves struck from north to south, according to an extensive new climate study.
items.content: Almost the entirety of Europe saw above-average temperatures last year, while wildfires burnt a record area and heatwaves struck from north to south, according to an extensive new climate study.

items.title: Train collision in Indonesia kills 14 as rescuers work to reach survivors
items.standfirst: Efforts continue to free two trapped passengers in wreckage after long-distance train collides with commuter train outside Jakarta, injuring 81The death toll from a train collision near the Indonesian capital Jakarta has risen to 14 with another 84 injured, the train operator said on Tuesday, as rescuers worked to extract survivors still trapped in the wreckage.The collision between a commuter train and a long-distance train happened late on Monday in Bekasi, just outside Jakarta. items.title: EU says Meta is failing to keep underage users off Facebook and Instagram
items.standfirst: The European Union accused Meta on Wednesday of failing to stop underage users from accessing Facebook and Instagram, in violation of the bloc's digital rules that require sites to protect minors.
items.content: The European Union accused Meta on Wednesday of failing to stop underage users from accessing Facebook and Instagram, in violation of the bloc's digital rules that require sites to protect minors.(Image credit: Jeff Chiu)

items.title: Meta found in breach of EU law for failing to keep children off platforms
items.standfirst: Commission says tech company does not have effective measures to keep under-13s off Facebook and InstagramThe tech company Meta has been found to be in breach of EU law for failing to prevent children under 13 from using its Facebook and Instagram platforms.Issuing the preliminary findings of a nearly two-year investigation, the European Commission said on Wednesday that Meta did not have effective measures in place to stop under-13s accessing its services. Continue reading...
items.content: Commission says tech company does not have effective measures to keep under-13s off Facebook and InstagramThe tech company Meta has been found to be in breach of EU law for failing to prevent children under 13 from using its Facebook and Instagram platforms.Issuing the preliminary findings of a nearly two-year investigation, the European Commission said on Wednesday that Meta did not have effective measures in place to stop under-13s accessing its services. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Historic homecoming' as endangered antelopes flown to Kenya from Czech Republic zoo
items.standfirst: The mountain bongo is a rare antelope endemic to Kenyan highland forests, with less than 100 in the wild.
items.content: The mountain bongo is a rare antelope endemic to Kenyan highland forests, with less than 100 in the wild.

items.title: 'Homecoming': Critically endangered antelopes returned to Kenyan forests from Czech zoo
items.standfirst: Four mountain bongos have arrived in Kenya after being returned from a Czech zoo in a bid to bolster numbers of the critically endangered species of which there are less that 100 left in the wild. Kenyan officials hailed the return as a “homecoming of the majestic bongos".
items.content: Four mountain bongos have arrived in Kenya after being returned from a Czech zoo in a bid to bolster numbers of the critically endangered species of which there are less that 100 left in the wild. Kenyan officials hailed the return as a “homecoming of the majestic bongos".

items.title: Champions League: PSG come out on top in legendary game against Bayern Munich
items.standfirst: Paris Saint-Germain beat Bayern Munich 5-4 in the first leg of the Champions League semi-final. With nine goals, it was the highest-scoring Champions League semi-final in history.
items.content: Paris Saint-Germain beat Bayern Munich 5-4 in the first leg of the Champions League semi-final. With nine goals, it was the highest-scoring Champions League semi-final in history.

items.title: PSG vs Bayern Munich: Football players, fans react to Champions League semi
items.standfirst: The nine-goal thriller in Paris is hailed as one of the best football matches across all competitions.
items.content: The nine-goal thriller in Paris is hailed as one of the best football matches across all competitions.

items.title: PSG edge Bayern in nine-goal Champions League semi-final thriller
items.standfirst: Khvicha Kvaratskhelia and Ousmane Dembélé struck twice each as Paris Saint-Germain survived a furious Bayern Munich comeback to win 5-4 in a sensational Champions League semi-final first leg on Tuesday, setting a new record for the highest-scoring match at this stage.
items.content: Khvicha Kvaratskhelia and Ousmane Dembélé struck twice each as Paris Saint-Germain survived a furious Bayern Munich comeback to win 5-4 in a sensational Champions League semi-final first leg on Tuesday, setting a new record for the highest-scoring match at this stage.
